mnemonics: RRS Formula: Remember the Recipe Steps: 1) Find Root (Homogeneous), 2) Find Particular (Non-homogeneous), 3) Sum Solution
keyPoints: A first-order linear recurrence relation has the form: aₙ = c * aₙ₋₁ + f(n)
The solution is the sum of the homogeneous solution (aₙ⁽ʰ⁾) and a particular solution (aₙ⁽ᵖ⁾)
The homogeneous solution, for constant c, is aₙ⁽ʰ⁾ = A * cⁿ, where A is a constant determined by the initial condition
commonMistakes: Forgetting to add the homogeneous and particular solutions together for the general solution
Using the initial condition on the homogeneous solution alone, instead of on the full general solution
Assuming the particular solution form incorrectly (e.g., not multiplying by n if f(n) is similar to the homogeneous solution)
